h1: How to Find Nursing Homes in Alabama (2026 Guide)
introText: This guide explains how to evaluate and find real nursing homes in Alabama — including quality metrics to check, questions to ask, and how to pay for care. For accurate, current facility information (names, addresses, CMS star ratings, and inspection history), use Medicare.gov Care Compare directly; it is the official CMS tool and is updated continuously.

qualityMetrics.description: Overall 1–5 star rating from CMS based on inspections, staffing, and quality measures.
Nursing hours per resident per day; higher staffing is generally associated with better outcomes.
Recent state survey findings, including any deficiencies or enforcement actions.
Clinical indicators such as fall rates, pressure ulcers, and infections.

howToChoose.title: Start with Medicare.gov Care Compare
howToChoose.description: Search by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified facility in your area, with star ratings and inspection reports.

howToChoose.title: Schedule in-person visits
howToChoose.description: Tour facilities at different times of day — including evenings and weekends — to observe staffing and resident engagement.

howToChoose.title: Ask about staff qualifications and turnover
howToChoose.description: High turnover is a red flag. Ask about staff training, ratios, and tenure.

howToChoose.title: Review contracts and costs carefully
howToChoose.description: Get a written breakdown of base rates, extra charges, and discharge/transfer policies.

howToChoose.title: Contact your state long-term care ombudsman
howToChoose.description: Ombudsmen can share complaint history and advocate on your behalf — the service is free and confidential.

faqs.question: Where can I find a list of licensed nursing homes in Alabama?
faqs.answer: The official list of Medicare- and Medicaid-certified facilities is maintained on Medicare.gov Care Compare. Alabama's state licensing agency also publishes its own list of currently licensed facilities.

faqs.question: How do I choose a nursing home in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Start with CMS star ratings on Medicare.gov Care Compare, visit facilities in person, review the most recent state inspection report, ask about staff ratios and turnover, and contact the Alabama long-term care ombudsman for complaint history.

sources.title: National Long-Term Care Ombudsman Resource Center

qualityMetrics.description: Overall 1–5 star rating from CMS based on inspections, staffing, and quality measures.
Nursing hours per resident per day; higher staffing is generally associated with better outcomes.
Recent state survey findings, including any deficiencies or enforcement actions.
Clinical indicators such as fall rates, pressure ulcers, and infections.

howToChoose.title: Start with Medicare.gov Care Compare
howToChoose.description: Search by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified facility in your area, with star ratings and inspection reports.

howToChoose.title: Schedule in-person visits
howToChoose.description: Tour facilities at different times of day — including evenings and weekends — to observe staffing and resident engagement.

howToChoose.title: Ask about staff qualifications and turnover
howToChoose.description: High turnover is a red flag. Ask about staff training, ratios, and tenure.

howToChoose.title: Review contracts and costs carefully
howToChoose.description: Get a written breakdown of base rates, extra charges, and discharge/transfer policies.

howToChoose.title: Contact your state long-term care ombudsman
howToChoose.description: Ombudsmen can share complaint history and advocate on your behalf — the service is free and confidential.

faqs.question: Where can I find a list of licensed hospice providers in Alabama?
faqs.answer: The official list of Medicare- and Medicaid-certified facilities is maintained on Medicare.gov Care Compare. Alabama's state licensing agency also publishes its own list of currently licensed facilities.

faqs.question: How do I choose a hospice provider in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Start with CMS star ratings on Medicare.gov Care Compare, visit facilities in person, review the most recent state inspection report, ask about staff ratios and turnover, and contact the Alabama long-term care ombudsman for complaint history.

sources.title: National Long-Term Care Ombudsman Resource Center

For accurate, current facility information (names, addresses, CMS star ratings, and inspection history), use Medicare.gov Care Compare directly; it is the official CMS tool and is updated continuously.

qualityMetrics.description: Overall 1–5 star rating from CMS based on inspections, staffing, and quality measures.
Nursing hours per resident per day; higher staffing is generally associated with better outcomes.
Recent state survey findings, including any deficiencies or enforcement actions.
Clinical indicators such as fall rates, pressure ulcers, and infections.

howToChoose.title: Start with Medicare.gov Care Compare
howToChoose.description: Search by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified facility in your area, with star ratings and inspection reports.

howToChoose.title: Schedule in-person visits
howToChoose.description: Tour facilities at different times of day — including evenings and weekends — to observe staffing and resident engagement.

howToChoose.title: Ask about staff qualifications and turnover
howToChoose.description: High turnover is a red flag. Ask about staff training, ratios, and tenure.

howToChoose.title: Review contracts and costs carefully
howToChoose.description: Get a written breakdown of base rates, extra charges, and discharge/transfer policies.

howToChoose.title: Contact your state long-term care ombudsman
howToChoose.description: Ombudsmen can share complaint history and advocate on your behalf — the service is free and confidential.

faqs.question: Where can I find a list of licensed hospice providers in Kansas?
faqs.answer: The official list of Medicare- and Medicaid-certified facilities is maintained on Medicare.gov Care Compare. Kansas's state licensing agency also publishes its own list of currently licensed facilities.

faqs.question: How do I choose a hospice provider in Kansas?
faqs.answer: Start with CMS star ratings on Medicare.gov Care Compare, visit facilities in person, review the most recent state inspection report, ask about staff ratios and turnover, and contact the Kansas long-term care ombudsman for complaint history.

sources.title: National Long-Term Care Ombudsman Resource Center

introText: This guide explains how to evaluate and find real hospice providers in New Mexico — including quality metrics to check, questions to ask, and how to pay for care. For accurate, current facility information (names, addresses, CMS star ratings, and inspection history), use Medicare.gov Care Compare directly; it is the official CMS tool and is updated continuously.

qualityMetrics.description: Overall 1–5 star rating from CMS based on inspections, staffing, and quality measures.
Nursing hours per resident per day; higher staffing is generally associated with better outcomes.
Recent state survey findings, including any deficiencies or enforcement actions.
Clinical indicators such as fall rates, pressure ulcers, and infections.

howToChoose.title: Start with Medicare.gov Care Compare
howToChoose.description: Search by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified facility in your area, with star ratings and inspection reports.

howToChoose.title: Schedule in-person visits
howToChoose.description: Tour facilities at different times of day — including evenings and weekends — to observe staffing and resident engagement.

howToChoose.title: Ask about staff qualifications and turnover
howToChoose.description: High turnover is a red flag. Ask about staff training, ratios, and tenure.

howToChoose.title: Review contracts and costs carefully
howToChoose.description: Get a written breakdown of base rates, extra charges, and discharge/transfer policies.

howToChoose.title: Contact your state long-term care ombudsman
howToChoose.description: Ombudsmen can share complaint history and advocate on your behalf — the service is free and confidential.

faqs.question: Where can I find a list of licensed hospice providers in New Mexico?
faqs.answer: The official list of Medicare- and Medicaid-certified facilities is maintained on Medicare.gov Care Compare. New Mexico's state licensing agency also publishes its own list of currently licensed facilities.

faqs.question: How do I choose a hospice provider in New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Start with CMS star ratings on Medicare.gov Care Compare, visit facilities in person, review the most recent state inspection report, ask about staff ratios and turnover, and contact the New Mexico long-term care ombudsman for complaint history.

introText: This guide explains how to evaluate and find real nursing homes in New York, NY. For the official list of CMS-certified facilities currently operating in New York — including star ratings, inspection reports, and staffing data — use Medicare.gov Care Compare directly.

faqs.question: How do I find nursing homes in New York?
faqs.answer: Search Medicare.gov Care Compare by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified nursing home serving New York, NY. The local Area Agency on Aging (findable via Eldercare Locator at 1-800-677-1116) can also provide referrals.

faqs.answer: Search Medicare.gov Care Compare by ZIP code to see every CMS-certified adult day care center serving Warren, MI. The local Area Agency on Aging (findable via Eldercare Locator at 1-800-677-1116) can also provide referrals.
